interactions.summary: Jiang answers the world-order question with three systemic trends: de-industrialization, mercantilism, and re-militarization, then uses Japan and China to illustrate who may adapt and who may cling to the dying order.
The interviewer compares China's attachment to the old order with Europe's and then asks how Russia will respond to a lawless environment and direct pressure from the West.
Jiang begins his Russia answer by saying Putin is the only leader with a grand strategy and is waiting for a U.S. ground invasion of Iran to trap Washington in a long quagmire.

speaker_notes.note: The transcript repeatedly mangles the word 'rapprochement,' but the meaning is clearly a temporary U.S.-China thaw.

claims.claim: Jiang says the next order will be shaped by three major trends: de-industrialization, mercantilism, and re-militarization.
Jiang defines de-industrialization as moving away from energy-hungry urban knowledge economies because cheap Middle Eastern energy is no longer dependable.
Jiang defines mercantilism as building local trade and self-owned supply networks because the global order is dead.
Jiang says re-militarization follows because Pax Americana is dead and Pax Judaica will not protect weaker states from predation.
Jiang predicts Japan will adapt earliest by mobilizing youth for sacrifice, self-sufficiency, re-militarization, and de-industrialized adjustment.
Jiang says China is less likely to adapt because it remains psychologically and materially attached to the old global order.
Jiang predicts a short-term U.S.-China rapprochement, including a friendly Trump visit to Beijing on March 31, even though he thinks the underlying global order cannot be saved.
claims.temporal_scope: Short-term forecast from 2026-03-09 about March 31, 2026 and the remaining months of the year.

claims.claim: Jiang says Putin is waiting for an American ground invasion of Iran because it would lock the United States into a five-to-ten-year quagmire like another Vietnam.
claims.temporal_scope: Strategic reading of Russia stated on 2026-03-09.

signature_moments.moment: If you do not re-arm and defend your space, you get eaten alive by a wolf.
signature_moments.source_phrase: you will be eaten alive by a wolf
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is Jiang's compressed moral for re-militarization in a post-American order.

signature_moments.moment: The powers may patch relations just long enough to repair a world order Jiang thinks is already dying.
signature_moments.source_phrase: there's no saving the global order. It's dying
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It captures the tension between tactical cooperation and structural collapse.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's prescription for a less energy-intensive, more self-sufficient economic order after Middle East breakdown.
Building local trade circuits and spheres of influence instead of trusting the dead global order.
A temporary thaw Jiang expects between Washington and Beijing despite deeper structural decay.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang makes a concrete dated forecast here: a Trump visit to Beijing on March 31, 2026 and a partial thaw within the remaining months of 2026.

uncertainty_notes.note: The interviewer's Valdai assassination theory is presented as speculation to motivate the Russia question, not as an established fact in the packet.
